CheckTitle: Every active Okta Password Policy locks accounts after 3 or fewer failed attempts
Description:
Every **active Okta Password Policy** must lock the account after at most `3` consecutive failed sign-in attempts.

Okta evaluates Password Policies by group assignment, so a permissive custom policy with a higher threshold (or no lockout) can govern users even when the default is compliant. The check emits one finding per active policy.
Risk:
A high lockout threshold (or no threshold) leaves accounts exposed to online password guessing.

- **Online brute force** retains enough attempts to enumerate common passwords
- **Credential stuffing** can iterate through breached credentials at scale
- **Inconsistent baselines** leave users on legacy policies with weaker thresholds than the default

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to the **Okta Admin Console** as a *Super Admin*.
2. Navigate to **Security** > **Authentication** > **Password**.
3. For every active policy, click **Edit** and set *Lock out user after X unsuccessful attempts* to `3` or fewer.
4. Save the policy.
5. Repeat for every active Password Policy returned by the check.
Code.Terraform:
```hcl
resource "okta_policy_password" "<example_resource_name>" {
  name                  = "<example_resource_name>"
  status                = "ACTIVE"
  password_max_lockout_attempts = 3  # Critical: STIG-aligned lockout threshold
}
```

Recommendation.Text:
Configure every active **Okta Password Policy** so:
- *Lock out user after X unsuccessful attempts* is `3` or fewer
- Group assignments do not route users to legacy policies with higher thresholds or no lockout

Review each active Password Policy individually — the check evaluates them one at a time.

Notes: Aligns with DISA STIG V-273189 / OKTA-APP-000170. Not applicable when Okta delegates password sourcing to an external directory (AD/LDAP) — mute the check in that case; the directory enforces lockout instead.
